results.title: How to write & conduct effective performance appraisals. V.1
results.brief_description: Program leader Jimmy Calano, provides a step-by-step seminar presentation where we learn to turn a dreadful task into a motivating tool. Performance appraisals are one of the toughest jobs in management, yet executed properly they are an important way to keep people growing. Some of the skills include how to identify over- and under-performing achievers, eight traditional performance appraisal methods and how to design an appropriate appraisal format.

results.title: How to windsurf
results.brief_description: This video is designed to teach windsurfing in seven lessons. Information on how to assemble the board, how to get underway, steering, tacking, gybing and a section concerning safety are included. The video is designed so that each lesson is self-contained. The lessons are aimed at beginners of any age.

results.title: How to use classroom films
results.brief_description: This film introduces the basic principles necessary for an effective presentation and utilisation of a classroom film. It illustrates methods of choosing and reserving a film, pre-viewing, preparing the class, and initiating a follow-up discussion.

results.creator_credit: Matthew A Keller and CareerTrack Publications
results.brief_description: This program shows how video can be used in training. Topics such as what to look for in video training, what kinds of programs are available, the benefits of training using video and how to set up an overall video training program.

results.title: How to Sell to Australia
results.brief_description: Since the beginning of television, Australian advertising has embedded itself into the national psyche through harnessing the moving image. Advertisers haven’t just sold products to Australian audiences, they’ve also built community, spread iconic catchphrases and created beloved characters.

constellations_primary.name: Short films that sell: the power of TV advertising
constellations_primary.description: The MTA’s "Dumb Ways to Die" has over 300 million YouTube views. It's tongue-in-cheek approach to reflects a unique way of advertising that cuts through and make an impact, like the other ads featured in this Constellation.

results.description: Advertising in Australia is unique, innovative and has a language all its own. Since the beginning of television in 1956 to today, Australian advertising has embedded itself into the national psyche by harnessing the power of the moving image. Through animation, live-action and apps, advertisers have been able to not just sell products to Australian audiences but also build community, spread iconic catchphrases and create beloved characters.
description_for_label: Advertising in Australia is unique, innovative and has a language all its own. results.title: How to run a lathe
results.brief_description: Based on the book “How to run a lathe”. Explains how the lathe is essential to construction of anything containing metal— cars, ships, planes etc. Goes on the explain the parts of the lathe and how to operate them. Aimed at operators and machinists rather then general interest.

results.title: How to read music: keyboard version
results.creator_credit: Harry Ratner, Frederick Noad, Fredereick Noad Productions and Omnibus Video
results.brief_description: An instructional video teaching the basics of reading music. The course covers: Pitch; Duration; Rests and ties; Familiar tunes; New time signatures; Sharps and flats; Chords and counterpoint; Both hands together. Frederick Noad, an outstanding all-round musician, takes you through each of the above points step-by-step with illustrated diagrams and demonstrations on the piano.

results.title: How to play cricket by Don Bradman
results.brief_description: This unique film was first produced in England in 1934 with the script written and spoken by Don Bradman. It has been updated with comments by Sir Donald, filmed at his home in Adelaide in May 1993. Provides a rare opportunity for cricket lovers to study the master batsman’s technique in detail, and is an invaluable coaching aid to any aspiring cricketer. Bradman’s test match career was an outstanding example of sporting brilliance; his career total of runs was 6,996 with an average of 99.9, including 29 centuries. Aggression, initiative and improvisation were the hallmarks of his playing and this footage, filmed on the London Cricket Ground in 1934, captures his style perfectly, shows the complete shot and stroke broken down into successive movements and as such provides an invaluable visual description of how to achieve maximum effectiveness and efficiency of movements when batting in a game.
